current_phase.label: Public /chat route verified and product acceptance closeout

current_milestone.label: Unified-account entry, onboarding, homepage, and one real DeepSeek round-trip are live

progress_summary: This repo remains the deployment wrapper for the official LobeHub image on the local Mac. The public `/chat` route recovered from the Docker Desktop outage after the release LaunchAgent was hardened to start Docker Desktop, run Compose, and check both 3210 and the OIDC sign-in bootstrap. A later DeepSeek-key disappearance was traced to a second unintended PostgreSQL bind mount under `~/codex/runtime/projects/lobechat/data/postgresql`; the real three-user database and encrypted DeepSeek key vaults were still present under the canonical project data root. The active runtime data was backed up and restored from the canonical `data/` directory, and the wrapper now treats `/Users/hernando_zhao/codex/projects/lobechat/data` as the only LobeHub persistent data root through explicit `LOBE_DATA_DIR` mounts for PostgreSQL, Redis, and RustFS. The deployment profile and LaunchAgent now point at the canonical project entrypoint, while accidental runtime helper calls redirect back to canonical so a stale runtime checkout cannot silently create another `hz-root`-style alternate database. The live stack has been restarted from canonical, PostgreSQL is mounted from the canonical data root, the database shows the three root-domain users with DeepSeek key vaults, and the real browser `/chat/` route reaches the authenticated LobeHub homepage. Existing product decisions remain in force: `/chat` uses root-domain OIDC, LobeHub email/password login stays disabled, mounted-path custom image patches remain required, and DeepSeek remains the only provider that has been live-validated end to end. The wrapper now also exposes the existing SearXNG JSON API on local loopback `127.0.0.1:18080` for stock-dashboard shortpick experiments that need model-planned web search without reusing LobeChat browser sessions, user memory, or workspace context.
blockers: Non-DeepSeek provider keys in `deploy/.env` remain placeholders, so Anthropic/OpenAI/Google model validation is still intentionally out of scope for this round.
The live app still emits low-priority asset noise such as missing `favicon-progress.ico` variants, but this does not block auth, onboarding, homepage delivery, or the validated DeepSeek chat path.
next_step: Keep `/chat` health checks and SearXNG JSON checks together when this wrapper is restarted or upgraded; stock-dashboard consumers should call only the loopback search API, not hidden browser conversations.
